creator: girgen@partitur.se
text:
PostgreSQL i updated to 7.1.

The PostgreSQL port now obeys hier(7) by default, which means that it
installs into:

/usr/local/bin
/usr/local/lib
/usr/local/include/pgsql

This means it is important that folks pkg_delete old installations, or
at least move it away. If somone doesn't want this, the old layout is
still available like this:

> make -DWITH_OLD_LAYOUT

The pgsql user's home defaults to PREFIX/pgsql, and the PGDATA dir
defaults to ~pgsql/data, but can all be changed after compilation by
modifying the passwd database and the .profile file.

Some ports will have to be modified to work with the new layout. Where
they now have LDFLAGS=-L${LOCALBASE}/pgsql/lib and
CFLAGS=-I${LOCALBASE}/pgsql/include, this should instead be
LDFLAGS="-L${LOCALBASE}/lib -L${LOCALBASE}/pgsql/lib" and
CFLAGS="-I${LOCALBASE}/include -I${LOCALBASE}/pgsql/include", or
similar...

Fix: The following files shall be cvs removed:
   pkg-deinstall \
   files/patch-ar \
   files/patch-as \
   files/patch-at \
   files/patch-au \
   files/patch-av \
   files/patch-ba \
   files/patch-bb \
   files/patch-bc \
   files/patch-bd \
   files/patch-be \
   scripts/configure \
   scripts/createuser

The following files shall be cvs added:
   files/dot.cshrc.in \
   files/patch-contrib-retep-build \
   files/patch-doc-makefile \
   files/patch-jdbc-build-xml \
   files/patch-jdbc1-statement

Apply this patch:
